lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: It is good to see you again! The Dorgeshuun Council would like to ask a favour of you, if you are interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this favour?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Surface-dwellers have been visiting the mines for some time now, but no Dorgeshuun has yet visited the surface.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: We are curious about the surface, and we are also worried about the dangers it poses. We know that Lumbridge is friendly but we are worried that the HAM group may be plotting against us.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: We are planning to send an agent to the surface and we would like you to act as a guide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll act as a guide.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: In order to get into the HAM base undetected you will both need to go in disguise. You should get two full sets of HAM robes. Once you have them, our agent will meet you in the cellar of Lumbridge castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Oh dear! Our agent is ready, but the Council will not let an expedition go ahead without a guide. If you reconsider, please let me know!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You must be the famous [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Pleased to meet you! I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I can't wait to see the surface! But first you need to get us both sets of HAM robes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the robes?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: How should I know? You're the surface-dweller! Maybe you could steal them from the HAM people or something.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We'll need two sets of full robes: shirt, robe, hood, cloak, gloves, boots and badge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you scared of the surface?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well... a bit. All our legends say it's a terrible place.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But that's a bad reason not to visit it. Maybe the legends are wrong. Or even if they're right, we should know for sure.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: And I've always wondered... what's beyond the next cave? What's at the end of every tunnel? The surface is a whole new world to explore, and I can't wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that mark on your forehead?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I, er, er...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: To be honest I don't know. It happened when...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: If you don't mind, I'd rather not talk about it now. Maybe once you've shown me some of the surface.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: See you soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not [player name].

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh well.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik without two sets of HAM robes

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hi [player name]! Have you got our disguises yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm so looking forward to seeing the surface. But the Council insists that I get the disguise first.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik with two sets of HAM robes

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hi [player name]! Have you got our disguises yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have two sets of robes!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You should pick up your pet thingy. It would only get in the way.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: All right, let's go! I can't wait to see all around Lumbridge!

sections.title: Talking to Zanik after giving her the disguise

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are we waiting for? Let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else for a bit.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: All right. I'll wait for you in the castle cellar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you going to put on your disguise?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: There's no need for that yet. Why don't you show me around Lumbridge first? I'm so excited about visiting the surface!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I, er...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I will tell you, [player name], but not just yet. Finish showing me around Lumbridge and then I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Okay!

sections.title: Talking to Zanik after dismissing her

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are you ready to finish our tour now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well then, let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh well.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik in the kitchen

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Where are we? I thought that ladder led to the surface.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We are on the surface! We're in Lumbridge Castle!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's so enclosed, it could almost be underground. Let's go outside, [player name]! I want to see the sun!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So this is the surface!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What's that? The giant light?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the sun.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's so bright! I can't look at it!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But it's amazing to think that there's nothing above me. Just the sun and the air and empty space, forever...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: For generations my people have lived with rock over our heads. But now I can see the sky!

sections.title: Trying to enter the H.A.M. Hideout

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We can't go into the HAM lair yet! I'm not wearing my disguise!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You said you'd show me around Lumbridge before we went down there, Azacor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going down anyway.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll wait for you back in the castle then.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin guard
lines.text: Surface-dweller! You may not pass through that door!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name]. I'd like to show you around the city, but the council has decided not to let any surface-dwellers in yet.

sections.title: Talking to a man or woman around Lumbridge
Talking to a man or woman again

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Keep away from me you dirty goblin!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: I don't know, what will be next? Cave goblins LIVING up here? Taking our jobs? I wouldn't want my daughter to marry a goblin!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm not sure a Dorgeshuun would want to marry a human!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: What rudeness! This is why they say goblins have no manners!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh, I've got nothing against goblins in their own place. I just don't think they ought to be wandering around in our city and making the place look bad.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Goblins wandering around Lumbridge and talking to the duke like they're normal people? I don't know what the world's coming to.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: It just makes me uncomfortable, is all. What if I was in bed and some horrible little goblin poked its head up through the floor?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We would never do that! We don't tunnel upwards and we're very careful not to damage human cellars.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: You cave goblins... don't you, you know, eat people?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh! Well, that's all right then.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Eeek! What is it?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: A cave goblin? Well, there goes the neighbourhood!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: It's all very well the duke signing a peace treaty with those creatures. Maybe that'll stop them attacking us.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: But for them to come wandering around the surface and mixing with decent people? I don't know, what's the world coming to?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We were never going to attack you! YOU nearly attacked US!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh, they may say they're civilised, but they're still goblins. You can't trust them. The only reason they're not a threat is that they're so stupid.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Stop talking about me like I'm not here!

sections.title: Talking to Duke Huracio a second time

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Hello again, Zanik, [player name]. I hope you are enjoying your tour. Perhaps someday a human can have a similar tour of your city.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: It's good to see a visitor from the Dorgeshuun! I am Duke Horacio.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Headman Ur-Tag sends his greetings.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Tell Ur-Tag that I wish to speak with him again. I think that our cities could profit from trading with one another, but there can be little trade if he does not allow visitors to enter the city.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I know! But the ruling council is so slow to make decisions. It's made up of old fogeys who are set in their ways.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Now then, child, there is room for caution and experience in politics.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Maybe when I return I can convince them to open the gates of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to get into the city too.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I wish I could show it to you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Someday I will, but I don't think the council will agree to open the doors any time soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the treaty.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: It basically defines the borders between our two societies. It's not usual for two states to be above one another rather than side-by-side!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Everything above ground, within the borders of Lumbridge, belongs to Lumbridge. Everything below ground belongs to the Dorgeshuun. Except for the cellars of our buildings, those belong to Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Of course both our underground systems might expand. Neither state can build new tunnels that are too close to the other's systems...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: This is boring. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard of any HAM activity lately?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: No... I know they've been gaining members but I haven't heard anything else from them. They may be planning something, but then again they may not.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: What, er, who is this? Is it a cave goblin?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik is the first cave goblin to visit the surface! I'm giving her a tour of Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, er, pleased to meet you, Zanik.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: In fact, it's a good thing I met you. It's the Duke's birthday today, and I should be making him a lovely big birthday cake. I need eggs, flour and milk...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't know what those things are! Are they exotic surface foods?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, they're not exactly exotic, they're just the basic ingredients of a cake.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: If they're the basic ingredients, why don't you already have them?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, er...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think Zanik has time to help you.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: It was worth a try. Anyway...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: How is the adventuring going, my friend?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Hello there Zanik! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What's that thing with the wheel?

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: This is my spinning wheel! Look at the way it goes round and round and round...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What's it for?

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: It spins wool into balls so we can work it more easily.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't know what wool is, but I'm sure it's fascinating!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: What about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Banker tutor
lines.text: Good day, how may I help you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Banker tutor
lines.text: This is the Lumbridge branch of the Bank of Gielinor! Would you like to access your account?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't have an account! All my belongings are safe at home in Dorgesh-Kaan!

lines.speaker: Banker tutor
lines.text: What about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Hans
lines.text: Hello. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm giving Zanik here a tour of Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Hans
lines.text: Pleased to meet you, Zanik! I know not everyone in Lumbridge was pleased with the peace treaty, but I'm delighted to have new underground neighbours.

lines.speaker: Gee/Donie
lines.text: Hello there, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm taking Zanik on a tour of Lumbridge. She's the first cave goblin to visit the surface!

lines.speaker: Gee/Donie
lines.text: Hello Zanik! I hope you like what you find up here!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's amazing! The sky is so huge!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But I'm not sure I'd want to live under it for too long. It's kind of scary!

lines.speaker: Gee/Donie
lines.text: You'd get used to it!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Greetings adventurer. I am Phileas the Lumbridge Guide.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Greetings Zanik! I am here to give information and directions to new players. Do you require any help?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No thank you! [player name] is showing me around!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, can I help you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Cooking tutor
lines.text: Hello there Zanik! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What kind of foods do you eat up here on the surface?

lines.speaker: Cooking tutor
lines.text: All sorts of foods! There are lots of kinds of fish, and there are stews, pizzas, bread, baked potatoes...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: This is so exciting! Perhaps later I'll come back and sample lots of different surface foods!

lines.speaker: Cooking tutor
lines.text: I'm sure you'll like them! What about you, [player name]? Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Get out of my shop! GET OUT!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: We don't serve the likes of that thing in here.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't need anything you've got to sell anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go, Zanik.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: And don't come back, you filthy goblin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not with her.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Oh, well... what do you want?

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Welcome to the church of Holy Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What's a church? And what is Holy Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Surely you have heard of the god, Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: He who creates the forces of goodness and purity in this world? I cannot believe your ignorance!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh, a GOD.

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Not just A god! Saradomin is the god with more followers than any other! At least in this part of the world.

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Which god do you cave goblins worship?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We DON'T worship any god!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thousands of years ago the gods made our ancestors fight in horrible wars. It was only after we escaped from them that we were able to build a civilisation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What god did the Dorgeshuun use to follow?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Our history does not remember his name. We know only that he was a violent god of war.

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Sounds like Zamorak to me! Or one of the minor gods who followed him.

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Saradomin would never be so barbaric.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened in the wars?

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: We do have stories about a war between the gods. I had always interpreted them as symbolic of the spiritual war between light and darkness in our hearts.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Dorgeshuun history remembers them as real. But we don't know much detail. Our ancestors were just footsoldiers, they didn't know what they were fighting for.

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: I am sure they were engaged in a noble struggle, although perhaps on the wrong side!

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Saradomin was fighting to bring goodness and order to the universe, but Zamorak was leading the forces of darkness in a bid to bring eternal night to the world!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Our history doesn't make a distinction. There was your own god who would kill you if you refused to fight, and someone else's god who would only kill you if you lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not all the gods are bad!

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Quite right! The Dorgeshuun have just had bad experiences of them.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Maybe that's true. I should try to approach things with an open mind!

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: Exactly! You Dorgeshuun have lived in darkness for too long. But now you can come into the light of Holy Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We don't like bright lights. They hurt our eyes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Father Aereck
lines.text: May Saradomin bless you in all your pursuits!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I can manage without Saradomin, but thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Woodsman tutor
lines.text: Greetings, Zanik! Listen to the wind in the trees...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are these trees then?

lines.speaker: Woodsman tutor
lines.text: Of course! What did you think they were?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't know! I've never visited the surface before!

lines.speaker: Woodsman tutor
lines.text: Shall I show you how to chop trees and make fires then?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Perhaps some other time. Right now I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Woodsman tutor
lines.text: How about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Smithing apprentice
lines.text: Hello there, Zanik! You look like you know your ores!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well, I know iron and silver. What other ores are there?

lines.speaker: Smithing apprentice
lines.text: There are all sorts! Mithril, adamantite, runite... shall I tell you about them?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Perhaps some other time.

lines.speaker: Smithing apprentice
lines.text: What about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do after smelting the ore?

lines.speaker: Smithing apprentice
lines.text: Ahh you'll need to learn to smith. You can use bronze bars on the anvil outside with a hammer.

lines.speaker: Smithing apprentice
lines.text: But for the best advice, go to Varrock. That's where my master, the Smithing Tutor, plies his trade. Ask him to teach you how to smith.

lines.speaker: Smithing apprentice
lines.text: Smelt some ore and store it in the bank. Grab a hammer from the general store before you go too!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Ranged combat tutor
lines.text: Hi, Zanik! I see you know how to handle a bow!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: They tell me I'm an expert with the bone crossbow!

lines.speaker: Ranged combat tutor
lines.text: You don't need any more help, then. How about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Magic combat tutor
lines.text: Greetings Zanik! You look like someone who might know something about spells!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Actually I don't know anything about spells.

lines.speaker: Magic combat tutor
lines.text: Well, if you have some air runes and mind runes you can cast Wind Strike...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Magic combat tutor
lines.text: You can't even cast Wind Strike?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We Dorgeshuun don't have any magical knowledge at all.

lines.speaker: Magic combat tutor
lines.text: Well, if you don't know the basics at all it'll take a long time to teach you...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Perhaps some other time then.

lines.speaker: Magic combat tutor
lines.text: How about you, [player name]? Do you want any help?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Melee combat tutor
lines.text: Ho there Zanik! You look like a strong fighter!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've been training on giant frogs!

lines.speaker: Melee combat tutor
lines.text: You don't need any more help, then. How about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Prayer tutor
lines.text: Greetings, Zanik! Dedication's what you need.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Prayer tutor
lines.text: Because training your prayer takes a long time!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't want to train prayer! I can manage without asking the gods for favours!

lines.speaker: Prayer tutor
lines.text: Suit yourself. How about you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: Can I help you at all?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's a surface shop full of exotic surface goods! What are you selling?

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: Um, yes, exotic surface goods... here, have a look at our wares...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Wow... The bucket and the hammer, what are they made of?

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: Um... wood. It, er, grows on trees?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Amazing!

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: So, do you want to buy anything?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: A wood bucket! And a wood hammer! And a newcomer map! I brought some surface money.

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: There you go!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: How about you?

sections.title: Walking near the goblins in the forest

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are those... goblins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: H-hello? Goblin?

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Who you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Dorgeshuun?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Yes! The Dorgeshuun tribe? We live underground. We've been cut off from other goblins for generations but now we're back!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We, er, we're your distant cousins? We want to learn about your culture.

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Culture?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Your art! Your philosophy! Your way of life! Now that the godwars are over you must have been able to build a peaceful civilisation!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Peace is for weak. We goblins! We strong!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Why is peace for the weak?

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Big High War God say so! He make us strong!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Big High War...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh. I see.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's go, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we come through this gate?

lines.speaker: Border Guard
lines.text: You may pass if you pay the toll, but your goblin friend must stay on this side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Border Guard
lines.text: Why do you think this gate is here, if not to keep the goblins out of Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Zanik isn't like other goblins!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Never mind, [player name]. I don't think we need to go to Al Kharid anyway!

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you doing on my land?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Your land?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Yes, my land! This is my farm!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm sorry! I didn't realize. I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: So you're one of them cave goblins, then? I suppose you weren't to know.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are those things outside?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Things? There's more than one now?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: There are lots of them! You know, the white fluffy things?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Oh, you mean my sheep!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: 'Sheep'! Finding all these strange surface creatures is so exciting!

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: But as for YOU...

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you doing on my land?

lines.speaker: Millie Miller
lines.text: Hello Adventurers. Welcome to Mill Lane Mill. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! What is this place?

lines.speaker: Millie Miller
lines.text: This is Mill Lane Mill. Millers of the finest flour in Gielinor, and home to the Miller family for many generations

lines.speaker: Millie Miller
lines.text: We take grain from the field nearby and mill into flour.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What's flour?

lines.speaker: Millie Miller
lines.text: It's the stuff we get from milling the grain. We use it to make bread and cakes and things.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We don't have any of those things in Dorgesh-Kaan! They sound so interesting!

lines.speaker: Father Urhney
lines.text: Go away! I'm meditating!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Father Urhney
lines.text: I don't care! I'm still meditating!

lines.speaker: Candle seller
lines.text: Do you want a lit candle for 1000 gold?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No thank you, I have a lantern of my own!

lines.speaker: Candle seller
lines.text: Oh! Who are you? Some kind of goblin?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Dorgeshuun live in the caves beneath the swamp. I made contact with them and now I'm giving Zanik a tour or the surface!

lines.speaker: Candle seller
lines.text: Oh, that's good! And it's all down to the candles I sold! So do you want to buy another one?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Hello Zanik, hello [player name]. How is your exploration of the surface going?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've hardly SEEN the surface yet! [player name] just dragged me back down here!

lines.speaker: Kazgar
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! Hello, surface-dweller! Would you like me to lead you to the mines?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hello, Kazgar!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I ought to visit the Duke of Lumbridge while I'm here. Ur-Tag asked me to give him his regards.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Duke Horacio seemed nice. But politics is just as boring up here as it is at home!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: There are so many people around! You must introduce me to them, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's a pity some people up here don't like me. I suppose I should have expected that.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But at least you're friendly, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Why don't you take me to a shop? I promised Tignik I would bring back a souvenir from the surface.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: This 'wood' is an odd material. It's heavier than bone but it doesn't seem to be as brittle. It would be good for making crossbows out of!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are there still goblins living on the surface, [player name]? Are there any near Lumbridge?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I should reunite the two sides of the race!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Those poor goblins. It's hard to believe I'm the same species as them. No wonder the HAM people hate us if they assume we're like them.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So far on the surface we haven't seen any... you know... gods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course not! The gods don't just wander around Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Don't they? I have no idea what to expect!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: That priest seemed friendly. I thought any servant of a god would be much more aggressive.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are we doing down here? I want to see the surface!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen enough of Lumbridge yet?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Perhaps we could talk to the Duke of Lumbridge. Headman Ur-Tag asked me to give him his regards.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You must introduce me to some of the surface people!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well... our legends all talk about the gods living on the surface, but since I've been up here I haven't see any. Is there anyone around here who might know about them?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are there still goblins living on the surface, Player? Are there any near Lumbridge?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I should reunite the two sides of the race!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I promised Tignik I would bring a souvenir back from the surface. Will you take me to a shop?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I think I've seen enough of Lumbridge now. If you like I'll tell you about the mark on my forehead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: ...And when they opened up the giant frog's stomach they found all the missing silver, and Mistag's brooch!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your stories have entertained me. I will let you into the cave for a short time.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I hope I get ranging XP!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Aaaah!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What -- what's happening?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is not normal for the Tears to have that effect.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I know! What happened?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is a sign. The power of the Tears has marked you for a special purpose.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I cannot tell you more now. That mark will soon fade, but someday it will glow again, and on that day you should return to me so that I can tell you more about your destiny.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Wow...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Ever since then I've wondered what the sign meant. But perhaps you will help me to find out, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've seen enough of Lumbridge now. Let's get to the HAM lair and see if they're up to anything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I'm not interested!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You don't care about me at all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you tell me about the mark on your forehead?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I, er...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I will tell you, Azacor, but not just yet. Finish showing me around Lumbridge and then I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: All right. But not here. Let's go back into Lumbridge town.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We're getting a bit far away from Lumbridge, [player name]. I'd like to see the rest of the world someday but not right now. Let's stick to Lumbridge for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going anyway.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: All right. I'll wait for you back in Lumbridge castle cellar.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik without the HAM disguise

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Aren't you going to put on your HAM disguise? You might be able to do without one but I don't want you giving me away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost my HAM disguise!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You'll have to find a new one from somewhere then.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik while in disguise
Talking to Zanik in the Lumbridge cellar

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are you ready to go to the HAM cave now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well then, let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh well.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are we waiting for? Let's get to the HAM hideout and find out what they're planning!

sections.title: Talking to HAM guard near the door

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Hello, brother, you fit in much better now that you're correctly attired. Before you stuck out like a sore thumb! Keep your eyes peeled though; I've heard there are thieves amongst us!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: I don't think I've seen you before! What's your name?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of--

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I mean, I'm Zanik!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Zanik? What kind of a name is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik isn't from round here.

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: It's good that we're getting members from further afield!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Is the monster problem very bad where you're from, Zanik?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh yes. My whole city is overrun by goblins.

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Goblins? That's terrible!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: You know, there are goblins living right underneath Lumbridge. And the duke is treating them as if they were people like you and me! He's even signed a peace treaty with them!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So what's your group planning to do about it?

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Johanhus doesn't tell his plans to ordinary members like us, but I've heard he and Sigmund are planning something big.

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: I don't think we'll have to worry about the cave goblin threat for much longer!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No... I'm sure the threat will be dealt with soon.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Listen to what that speaker's saying, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: And they're all lapping it up! No one challenges him or even asks questions. They're just sitting there while he fills their minds with hate!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: So you've brought a friend down here to mock me, [player name]? Well soon I will be the one mocking you!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Is that Sigmund? The one who tried to start the war between Lumbridge and the cave goblins?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Yes, I am! And I'm not ashamed of it! Those cave goblins are an abomination in the eyes of Holy Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: But I have another plan to destroy them. Soon Johanhus and I will be meeting to discuss the details.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: And there's nothing you can do about it!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Welcome brother... to our organisation... Ours is a fight for humanity in all its shapes and forms... and to rid mankind of the monsters that exist in our world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you planning to do anything about the cave goblins?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: You should know, brother... we don't let ordinary members in on all our plans... but rest assured those foul creatures will be dealt with very soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you please tell me?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: No! That kind of information is for...only senior members of the group. Ordinary members like you must learn to...trust your leaders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it really necessary?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: How can you even...ask that? Right beneath Lumbridge is a whole cave system full of those creatures! They've got a passage leading right up into the castle kitchen!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: And I've heard that there's a whole city of those things down there, not that anyone has ever seen it. What have they got in there? What are they planning?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik without talking to all the HAMs

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So this is the HAM hideout. It's so squalid and dirty! I don't know how they can bear to live here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen enough?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We ought to talk to some of the HAM people. That's what we're here for, after all!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's have another look around the main area. Maybe there's something we missed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen enough?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Not yet. There must be another area where the HAM people make their plans. We should try to find it.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Look, there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Over there. There's a trapdoor hidden by dirt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You cave goblins must have better eyesight than humans. I would never have noticed it without you!

sections.title: Trying to pick the lock without 23 Thieving

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Can't you open it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not good enough at thieving.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm not much good at thieving either, I'm afraid. You'd better get some practice.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik inside the trapdoor

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The room where the HAM members make their plans must be down here somewhere. We've got to find it without getting caught!

sections.title: Getting spotted by the first guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! Ordinary members aren't allowed here!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Go back upstairs and I'll say nothing about it. But if any of the other guards see you you're in big trouble!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: *whispers* Get him to face away from me...

sections.title: Talking to Zanik after getting spotted

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: *whispers* Get that guard to turn his back on me. Maybe if you walk around him he'll keep facing you.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Ordinary members aren't allowed in this area. Get back to the cave above.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! Where do you think you're going?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Uh!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We can get through this if we work together, [player name]. But I can't get too close to the guards or they'll see I'm not human.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You've got to get them to turn their backs on me so I can shoot them without them seeing me draw my crossbow.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik after seeing her snipe the guard

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: If you can get to the other side of that guard you can make him turn around so I can shoot him. Make sure another guard doesn't catch you though!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait here.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Okay, let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't shooting people in the back wrong?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Why more so than shooting them in the front?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you kill that guard in one hit? I want to learn how to do that!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's a special technique with the bone crossbow. If we both get out of this alive I'll teach you, [player name]!

sections.title: Getting spotted by the guard on the left
Getting spotted by the guard on the right
Getting spotted by the patrolling guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What are you doing here!?

sections.title: Getting spotted by the guard at the door
Trying to talk to the patrolling guard

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name]! Wake up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bwuh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uuuurgh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The guards caught you but I managed to slip away. Here, I'll let you out!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's go!

sections.title: Trying to squeeze through a crack without 23 Agility

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Can't you get through?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not agile enough.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't think I could get through either. You'd better get some agility training done.

sections.title: Dealing with the second & third guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! What are you--

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Unk!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thanks, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Is the coast clear to get past?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, there's a guard patrolling the corridor.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll take care of him! Just tell me when his back is to us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We got him, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Noo! He's seen us!

sections.title: Three down, two more to go

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are two more guards but I don't know how to get behind them.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Then maybe you could lead one of them past me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are two more guards but I don't know how to get behind them.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: That guard in the east corridor... if I wait at the end of the corridor then you could lead him past me...

sections.title: Luring the fourth guard towards you

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Another one down!

sections.title: Deciding the plan for the final guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's just one guard left.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't think that guard's going to move away from the door. I think we need to approach him from different directions...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! You there!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: That's the last of them! Now to listen to what's happening in the meeting room!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no more guards! We've got them all!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I think I can hear something happening behind the double doors. We should listen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't hear anything.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Shh! I can hear them.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: They're saying something about... they're planning something... something about a machine...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh! Oh no!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are they saying?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We've got to do something, [player name]! They're going to--

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Got you, you spy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oww... my head...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's Zanik?

lines.speaker: Jimmy the Chisel
lines.text: Hello mate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see what happened to my friend?

lines.speaker: Jimmy the Chisel
lines.text: There was a lot of commotion when they dragged you in here. There was someone else, she was struggling like anything. They said they'd take her outside. Dunno what happened to her then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no! I've got to find her!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Hello, brother, you fit in much better now that you're correctly attired. Before you stuck out like a sore thumb! Keep your eyes peeled though; I've heard there are thieves amongst us!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Did you hear? They caught a goblin spy down in the store rooms!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you do to her?

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: Oh, Sigmund and some of the guys took it upstairs and dealt with it. Can't have goblins coming down here!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Guard
lines.text: You should have seen how it was screaming for mercy! Those monsters aren't so tough when they're outnumbered twenty to one!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: So, you got out of the cell? No matter...your goblin friend has already been dealt with...and Sigmund is on his way to deal with the rest of her foul brood!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik! Where are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik? Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Juna told Zanik that when the mark glowed again she should go to see her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I should take Zanik's body to Juna. I can't leave her here anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'll have to free up an inventory slot first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't just leave Zanik's body lying around! I should take her to Juna.

sections.title: Attempting to use alchemy spells on Zanik
Talking to Juna about Zanik's fate

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik is dead!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: That cannot be. She had not yet returned to learn of her destiny. The mark that the tears gave her had not begun to glow again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's glowing now. Look.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: This is indeed strange. I must commune with mighty Guthix and the forces of destiny.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Zanik's death was premature. The gods will allow it to be reversed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can just bring her back to life?!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The Tears of Guthix have powers beyond the understanding of mortals. They can restore her to life, with the gods' permission.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must collect some of the tears using Zanik's tear-bowl. Although I would not normally deem you worthy to collect the tears, I will let you into the cave for this special purpose.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must collect some of the tears using Zanik's tear-bowl. I will not count this against the next time you can collect the tears for yourself.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must have both hands free to carry the bowl. Speak to me again when your hands are free.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Twenty tears should be sufficient. Collect twenty blue tears, but remember that green tears will decrease the amount of blue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Then the forces of destiny must wait until a time that is more convenient for you.

sections.title: Talking to Juna before collecting the tears

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Zanik has not yet fulfilled her destiny, so the gods will allow her to be resurrected. You must collect some of the Tears of Guthix to allow this to happen.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must collect some of the tears using Zanik's tear-bowl. Although I would not normally deem you worthy to collect the tears, I will let you into the cave for this special purpose.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must collect some of the tears using Zanik's tear-bowl. I will not count this against the next time you can collect the tears for yourself.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Are you ready?

sections.title: Trying to exit Tears of Guthix before collecting all the tears

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You will need to collect twenty tears in order to resurrect Zanik.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to get out now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the Tears.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Then I will call upon their power.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Uuuhhh!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Get off me, you monsters!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are you doing? Noo! Don't kill me! [player name], help me!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: They killed me, didn't they, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Then what am I doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Juna brought you back from the dead.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Listen to me, Zanik. You are marked for a special purpose, and you have been granted new life so that you can carry it out. Your destiny is to lead all goblins into a new age.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But what does that mean?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Someday you will know. But today you must hurry to foil the plot that even now threatens your home!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The plot! [player name], I've got to tell you what I heard outside the meeting room!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't hear anything.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Shh! I can hear them.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Good news, brothers! Our tunnel from the water mill cellar has reached the cave goblin tunnels, and the last shipment of machinery is on its way from Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Do we have to deal with the dwarves? They may not exactly be monsters but they're still not human.

lines.speaker: HAM member
lines.text: If it wasn't for the King's tolerance of monsters, we humans would have a better industry than the dwarves!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Deacon
lines.text: Well said, brother!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: I'll need to take some of the low-level members to help unload the machinery at the Lumbridge water mill.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: By all means. It's about time that lot made themselves useful.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Soon the mighty River Lum will wash away those evil goblins forever!

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Deacon
lines.text: Saradomin will surely bless us for destroying those abominations!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Death to the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's go, [player name]! We've got to get to the Lumbridge water mill so we can foil Sigmund's plan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else for a bit.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Do it quickly, [player name]! We've got to stop Sigmund from destroying my city!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did Sigmund do to you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It was horrible, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: They didn't just k-kill me quickly. They were enjoying themselves. The light was so bright I couldn't see but I could hear them laughing...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's get them, [player name]! I need to save my city and I want to get my revenge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being dead?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It was... it wasn't like anything.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: One moment Sigmund and his thugs were... you know...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: And then suddenly I was down in the cave and you were here. There was nothing in between.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We Dorgeshuun don't believe in an afterlife. At least, if there is one we don't claim to know about it. And if anything happened to me while I was dead then I don't remember it.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: And now my life's a mystery too. What was that destiny Juna talked about?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But there's no time for that now. Let's get to the water mill!

sections.title: Talking to Zanik after dismissing her

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Quick, [player name]! Let's get to the water mill and stop Sigmund from destroying my home!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh well.

sections.title: Attempting to go back into HAM hideout

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are we going down there again for? We know where we need to go -- the Lumbridge water mill!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going down anyway.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll wait for you back in the castle then.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik at the mill

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We're not too late! They're still assembling the machine. Now we've got to find some way to get down there and stop them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can we get down there?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't know. But there must be something around here we can use...

lines.speaker: Kazgar
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! Hello, surface-dweller! Would you like me to lead you to the mines?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We've not got time to talk now, Kazgar! [player name], you've got to take me to the Lumbridge water mill at once!

sections.title: Talking to the dwarf at the mill without a disguise

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's in these crates?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: None of your business! Those funny pink-robed people told me to keep it secret!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's Sigmund?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: The guy who ordered all this? I don't see that's any of your business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you helping the HAM people?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: What, these odd pink-robed people? Oh, the normal reason. Because they paid me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But don't you know what they're planning to do?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: No, and I don't want to. Now go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to dwarf while in disguise

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, it's another one of you funny people. Okay, grab one of the crates and take it downstairs. I told your boss how to put the parts together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's in these crates?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Didn't your boss tell you? It's a digging machine. Very special. Digs straight up, and can work without supervision when it's turned on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's Sigmund?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: What, your boss? He's downstairs waiting for you slackers to carry the rest of the crates down. So get a move on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you helping the HAM people?

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Is that what you people call yourselves? Well, it's certainly not out of the goodness of my heart. Your boss paid good money for this machine, plus my fee for transporting it all this way.

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: So get to work and get it downstairs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no way I'm helping!

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, honestly! If you're not here to help then get out of the way!

sections.title: Talking to HAM member without a disguise

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Member
lines.text: *pant* Go away, outsider! *pant*

sections.title: Talking to HAM while in disguise

lines.speaker: H.A.M. Member
lines.text: These crates are heavy! I could do with some help if that's what you're here for.

sections.title: Trying to enter the trapdoor without a disguise

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Where do you think you're going? Only people helping assemble the machine are allowed down there!

sections.title: Trying to enter the trapdoor while Zanik is following you

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Hey! No goblins allowed down there!

sections.title: Trying to enter the trapdoor while disguised without Zanik

lines.speaker: Dwarf
lines.text: Hey! What do you think you're doing going down there empty-handed? Get a crate and carry it down!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Those must be the parts for the machine they're going to use to flood my city!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey... if I carried one of these crates I could slip downstairs and stop them!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Don't you dare go down there without me, [player name]! Find some way to get us both down there so I can face Sigmund myself!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Are you thinking what I'm thinking, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, what are you thinking?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I could hide in one of those crates, and then you could carry me downstairs as if you were carrying some of the machinery.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You ought to free both your hands so you can carry the crate. I'm afraid I might be heavy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt it.

sections.title: Carrying Zanik away from the mill

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Why are we going away from the mill? We should get down there and save my city!

sections.title: Attempting to summon a pet whilst carrying the crate
Trying to unlock the water mill tunnel

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: This must lead to the Dorgeshuun mines! Sigmund must mean the water to run through here and flood the city!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We've got to stop him, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from melee!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from magic!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from ranged!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from this foul goblin!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: [player name]! You and your goblin friend have interfered for the last time! Guards, kill them!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I won't let you destroy my home, Sigmund!

sections.title: Trying to smash the machine while Sigmund is undefeated
Talking to Zanik during the fight

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's get them, [player name]!

sections.title: Trying to leave the cellar whilst Zanik is still fighting

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Don't leave me, [player name]! I can't defeat Sigmund without you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll stay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be back!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hurry, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name]! Thank goodness you're all right! Now let's finish off Sigmund and his cronies before they flood the city!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Curse you, Player, and your goblin friend! You win this time, but we will meet again!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What happened? He vanished!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He must have had a ring of life! It looks like he got away from us this time, Zanik.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The important thing is we stopped him! Now let's smash that machine and then get back to Dorgesh-Kaan!

sections.title: Talking to Zanik before destroying the machine

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Quick, [player name], let's smash that drilling machine before Sigmund returns! Then we can return to Dorgesh- Kaan for a hero's welcome!

sections.title: Trying to unlock the tunnel before destroying the machine

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We can't just leave that machine there for someone else to complete! We should smash it beyond repair before returning home!

sections.title: Trying to leave via the ladder

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: There's no need to go that way, [player name]! We can go south along the tunnel to the Dorgeshuun mine.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Now let's get back to Dorgesh-Kaan to tell them what happened! We can go south along the tunnel Sigmund has dug.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik after destroying the machine

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We did it, [player name]! My city is safe! Now we can follow that tunnel south back to the mines!

sections.title: Leaving and returning to the water mill cellar

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Where did you go? We should follow the tunnel south to Dorgesh-Kaan!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll show you the way through to the city. Wait till we tell Mistag about what happened!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Welcome back, Zanik. What have you to report?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The city is safe! The HAM group was plotting against us, but we stopped them!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: You stopped them this time, but the surface still poses a danger. The council has been debating how much contact we should have with it, and wants you to make a recommendation.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The surface is a strange and wonderful place! We should embrace it, not shut ourselves off!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: And not all surface-dwellers are bad. Without [player name] the city would have been destroyed for sure!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: [player name], we are in your debt once again.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: The council has voted that, if Zanik's recommendation was positive, humans from Lumbridge should be allowed into the city. You may now enter Dorgesh-Kaan!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: This is brilliant! I'm sure you won't regret this!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thank you for taking me on that adventure, [player name]! I'll show you how to use the bone crossbow like I promised I would!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Come find me in the city if you ever want to chat, [player name]. I'm going to go and think about what Juna said about my destiny. I don't know what it will involve, but I have a feeling we will adventure together again!
